titleStmt.title: SII 18.15: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 15. (B.K. No. 17 of 1933-34.) BYĀḌGI, BYADGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab set up in the temple of Siddhēśvara.

summary.p: This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Akālavarsha, is dated in Śaka 823, Durmati, corresponding to A.D. 901-02. It registers a gift made by Piṭṭamme when Lōkaṭe was governing Banavāsi Twelve-thousand. It states that the stone was set up by Asagabbe, the mother of Niṭṭallara Māramma, when Chikkaṁbara Bijja was the nāḷgāvuṇḍa of Sattiyaḷge Seventy and Budda was the ūrgāvuṇḍa.
Lōkaṭe of this record is apparently the same as Lōkaṭe or Lōkāditya of the Chellakētana family who is stated in other records to be governing Banavāsi-nāḍu as a subordinate of Kṛishṇa II.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī Akālavariṣa śrīprithuvīvallabha mahārājā
dhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭārarā pravarddhamāna
vijayarājyābhivṛddhi Uttarōttaraṁ nale śakanṛpakā
lātītasaṁvatsaraśataṅgaḷeṇṭunūṟairppattamuṟaneyā
durmmati Emba variṣaṁ pravarttise lōkaṭe banavāsi
pannirchchāsiravuvanāḷe piṭṭamme jātava biṭṭa
cikkaṁbara bijja sattiyaḷgeḷpattarkkaṁ nāḻgāvuṇḍu
geyye buddhanūrggāvuṇḍu geyye niṭṭallara mārammana
bbe Asagabbe Ī kallaṁ niṟisidaḷ Īdanaḻida
vāraṇasivaduḷ sāsira kavileyuṁ sāsirbbar
pārvvaruvaṁ kondōna lōkake sandōrappar yasya
yasya yadā bhūmi tasya tasya tadā phalaṁ svadattaṁ pa
radattaṁ vā yō harēti vasundharaṁ ṣaṣṭiṁvarṣasaha
srāṇi viṣṭāya jāyatē krimiḥ
Digital edition of SII 18.15 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
